title: Vibrant Mexican Peas Elote Bowl
description: A fermented-inspired, shattering quick-fix Mexican bowl with peas, toasted corn, sesame, and zesty lime that is perfect for a Buddhist temple-friendly meal.
instructions: Heat a dry skillet over medium heat and toast the corn kernels until golden brown, about 5 minutes.
Drain the soaked peas and rinse them under cold water.
Bring a pot of salted water to a boil, add the peas, and simmer for 10 minutes until tender.
Drain the peas and set them aside to cool slightly.
In a bowl, combine lime juice, sea salt, grated ginger, toasted sesame oil, gochujang, and a splash of water, stirring until smooth.
Toss the warm peas and toasted corn with the sauce, ensuring even coating.
Transfer the mixture to a serving bowl and sprinkle with chopped cilantro, toasted sesame seeds, and roasted coconut flakes.
Serve immediately for a burst of fermented‑like flavors and a shattering crunch.
